answers.p5q7: XAI either makes people trust AI more OR helps them trust right amount. Found more proof it just makes blind trust.
answers.p5q10: Worry explanations trick people into trusting bad AI. Because they found explanations mostly increase blind following.
answers.p5q4: Tables: XAI no help. Text: XAI big help. Text easier for people brains than number tables.
answers.p5q9: There was a study or two that showed confidence scores make less AI following. There was another one which said that explanations make more AI following even when wrong.
answers.p5q2: Compares XAI vs people alone (XAI better) and XAI vs AI alone (same). Can't say explanations really help extra.

answers.p4q6: People want to think hard about AI but can't always do it. Breaks down when they want good work but don't know how to check AI or have no time.
answers.p4q10: Stewardship means humans guide AI but own the final work. Humans still responsible, not sharing blame with AI.
answers.p4q8: Can't understand the topic good, can't fix AI bad answers, no time. Most people said checking AI answers was hardest.
answers.p4q4: Less work making stuff, more work judging what AI made. Now people just watch over AI like bosses.
answers.p4q2: RQ1 is when do people think critically with AI. RQ2 is when does AI make thinking easier/harder. One is about doing it, other about effort.

answers.p6q9: Teachers should give less work and more time since pressure makes kids use ChatGPT. Also teach them to use it as help, not to cheat.
answers.p6q2: Two ideas: kids who care about grades might use ChatGPT more OR less. Less won because they're scared of getting caught.
answers.p6q10: Too much ChatGPT makes students lazy with their brains so they forget stuff. They don't think hard enough to remember things.
answers.p6q4: Started 12 questions, experts said 10 were good. Then math test dropped 2 bad ones (4 and 9). Left with 8 okay ones.
answers.p6q7: Workload and time pressure went through ChatGPT to cause procrastination, bad memory, and worse grades. Worry about grades only affected procrastination and memory.

responses.p4comments: took a couple times to guess and check mc answers until they were marked right

responses.p6comments: Spent a lot of time reading, had to rush the answers

finalResponses.fq_like_most_least: I think highlights were in general helpful
finalResponses.fq_missing_confusing_unnecessary: I didn't do time mangement very well which caused a couple sessions to feel rushed

answers.cpq2: Less effort doing work, more effort checking AI. AI does fast first draft so people just review.
answers.cpq4: Students use ChatGPT when busy,  put off work + forget stuff. Skip real learning for easy answers.
answers.cpq6: Trust in AI. if someone trust it lots, don't check it. IF someone trusts themselves more = check it.
answers.cpq8: Busy schoolwork makes ChatGPT shortcut tempting. Paper shows pressure directly causes more use.
answers.cpq10: AI helps scores (XAI/students) but people check less + trust blind + lose memory skills.
